results.justification: Spike No More addresses same artefact (gradient spike during pretraining) via spectral analysis of Jacobian sub-layer ratios — same effect (spike dampening), different mechanism (static sub-layer:shortcut ratio vs QAJAQ's per-layer learnable coefficient).

results.matched_effect_cluster: GPAS learnable per-layer activation scaling with gradient-preserving gate
results.justification: GPAS introduces learnable per-layer SiLU-gate that scales activations while gradients flow unimpeded via stop-gradient — the GPAS scalar is conceptually identical to QAJAQ's α_ℓ (per-layer learnable coefficient). Different signal (activation vs gradient) but same artefact + functional effect.

results.matched_effect_cluster: Keel Post-LN highway residual for depth-stable gradient flow
results.justification: Keel highway connection preserves gradient flow across depth — depth-graded gradient stability, same artefact (gradient through depth), conceptually adjacent to QAJAQ's depth-anti-correlated coefficient.
Peri-LN explores per-layer normalization placement variants for layer-graded stabilization — same artefact + adjacent effect.
Per-example normalization-layer gradient noise prediction — uses per-layer information but as DIAGNOSTIC not as control coefficient.
Global clip-by-norm — different artefact level (global, not per-layer).
Depth-aware re-architecting — adjacent but different mechanism (post-hoc layer reordering).
Progressive Gradient Flow uses per-step gradient-noise schedule for sparsity training — adjacent gradient-flow control via scheduling, same artefact.
